commands.repair_reason: - [P2] Concrete file-level token lifecycle and security-boundary blockers remain; an automated repair can attempt them before maintainer/security acceptance.; Needs attention: The token-binding direction is security-positive, but partial lifecycle auth, stale token reuse, cleanup gaps, and leftover mutable scope headers need attention before merge.; Review finding: [P1] Accept scoped tokens on all lifecycle methods... commands.review_summary:
The PR changes MCP loopback auth to issue server-side scoped bearer tokens for gateway/agent context, updates CLI runner MCP env handling and Claude live-session reuse, and adds regression coverage for spoofed loopback scope headers.

PR surface: Source +133, Tests +165. Total +298 across 10 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Current main and v2026.6.8 source still thread mutable MCP loopback scope headers into tool context after bearer authentication, and the canonical security issue provides a concrete curl-shaped source reproduction.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Concrete file-level token lifecycle and security-boundary blockers remain; an automated repair can attempt them before maintainer/security acceptance.
